title: perception
description: PROPOSED (Phase F / G3). Robot -> client structured world-state, emitted at a LOW rate (~2-10 Hz, decoupled from the 50 Hz control loop) by an on-Pi detector running as a separate MEDIA-SIDE sidecar (never in the servo loop, never a :7777 control client) — the media bridge merges it onto the operator data channel alongside daemon telemetry. Gives a running script eyes to close a loop on (robot.perceive()), distinct from the human-facing video track and the laptop-side one-shot LLM vision. Additive (no protocol_version bump): older clients ignore an unknown type. See docs/phase_f_perception.md and daemon_followups.md §3.

ts_ns.description: Pi monotonic_ns when the frame was captured (same clock as telemetry.ts_ns), so a client can reject stale detections.

source.description: Which camera / composite tile produced the detection, e.g. "front", "wrist_right". Lets a script disambiguate views (the same gap the operator-set camera_layout fills for LLM vision).

objects.description: Detected objects, possibly empty (an explicit "nothing seen" — distinct from no frame arriving).

bbox.description: Normalized [x, y, w, h] in the source frame, each in [0,1] (x,y = top-left). Present when the detector is 2D.

xyz.description: Object centroid in ROBOT-BASE frame, meters [x, y, z]. Present only when the detector has depth/registration; absent for a monocular 2D detector.

id.description: Stable track id across frames when the detector tracks; absent for stateless per-frame detection.
